entry.title: Chart data label auto change color disabled in design theme
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new to SAC and I unfortunately disabled the auto color change of data labels when I selected a color for my data labels in my design theme. Back then I did not know that I would disable that function that way. Of course I have now the problem that in some charts the data labels are not readable as the background color is sometimes bright and sometimes dark. And for some unknown reason SAC does not allow you to change the color of different data labels to different colors in the same chart.

Does anybody know how i can revert the selected color in the design theme so that the auto color function works again? Normally there is a reset button to reset the color changes. However this button does not exist in the design theme. I really do not want to start my design theme from the beginning as I have already made so many changes to it.

I hope someone can help me.

entry.title: Re: How You Can Benefit from Cloud-Ready Mode in SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Dear Marcel,

We improved performance for single process in cloud-ready and executed diverse performance tests. As product management team I also tested a BP with hundreds addresses and/or relationships such as contact persons and did not see any performance issue. For tons of data you can use mass process with import & export functionality. 

Best regards, Sungmin

entry.title: 1 Jahr SAP Change Basecamp: Der Change Management Kompass durch den (SAP) Transformations-Dschungel
entry.content:
Befindest du dich gerade selbst im SAP Projekt Dschungel und möchtest den damit einhergehenden Wandel sinnhaft und nachhaltig vorantreiben?
Dann könnte das SAP Change Basecamp genau das richtige für dich sein, um dir praktische Orientierungshilfen für deinen Projektalltag als Change Manager:in anzueignen.

Wie eignet man sich die notwendigen Kenntnisse und Fähigkeiten beim Thema OCM an? 

Etappe 1: Eine Reise beginnt mit einer Vision

Da es nicht nur dir, sondern vielen anderen ebenso geht, dass der Weg durch den SAP Dschungel Orientierung bedarf und häufig rein technisch betrachtet wird, ist die Idee für das SAP Change Basecamp  entstanden. Visionäre Transformationsprojekte und eine veränderte IT-Landschaft bieten viele Chancen ein Unternehmen weiterzuentwickeln und zu digitalisieren. Doch ein IT-System ist nur so gut, wie die effektive Nutzung der Anwendenden es zulässt. Daher sind die Ansatzpunkte für ein effektives Change Management entscheidend, um eine solche Vision Realität werden zu lassen.

Organizational Change Management (kurz: OCM) ist dementsprechend ein essenzieller Bestandteil, um Mitarbeitende einzubinden und eine gewinnbringende Nutzung des neuen Systems zu erreichen. Dabei ist nebensächlich in welcher Transformation du gerade steckst, ob in SAP S/4HANA Projekten oder in einer Implementierung von weiteren SAP Lösungen wie die Einkaufssoftware Ariba oder das Personalmanagementsystem SuccessFactors.

Durch das frühzeitige Einplanen von OCM kann sichergestellt werden, dass nicht nur der Weg eine im positiven Sinne aufregende Reise wird, sondern auch, dass auftretende Turbulenzen kontrolliert überstanden werden und Betroffene zu Beteiligten werden.

Wie ist diese Reise gestartet?

Etappe 2: Eine gut durchdachte Reiseroute als Grundlage für das Basecamp

Bevor das erste SAP Change Basecamp gestartet ist, wurde es im Digital Change & Adoption Team in der SAP Beratung erprobt und dabei mit dem Repertoire aus unterschiedlichen Projekteerfahrungen und Industriewissen angereichert. Nach der internen Ausgestaltung wurde das Format 2023 extern pilotiert, sodass weiteres Feedback eingearbeitet werden konnte, um zukünftigen Teilnehmenden eine bestmögliche Erfahrung bieten zu können.

Welche Erfahrungen konnten gemeinsam gemacht werden?

Etappe 3: Das SAP Change Basecamp als Kompass durch den Projekt Dschungel.

Um konkrete Fragestellungen aus dem Projektalltag aufzugreifen und die Möglichkeit zu bieten auch ohne Vorkenntnisse Fähigkeiten im Change Management in SAP Projekten aufzubauen und diese zu erproben, bietet das SAP Change Basecamp genau diesen benötigten Erfahrungsraum.

Innerhalb von zwei Tagen lernt man die wichtigsten Stationen eines Change-Prozesses nach der SAP ACTIVATE Methodologie über Impulse von Change Management Berater:innen kennen und wendet diese in praxisnahen Übungen an.

Das Format umfasst unter anderem Themen wie Leadership Alignment, Stakeholder Management, Change Impact Analyse und Change Effektivitätsmessung. In Kleingruppen werden kurze Exkursionen zu den jeweiligen Themen unternommen und mit jedem Stopp wird etwas Neues über die Methodik erlernt. Begleitet von einer Fallstudie können alle Inhalte anhand eines fiktiven SAP S/4HANA Kundenprojektes angewandt, reflektiert und diskutiert werden.

Zusätzliche gibt es Einblicke, wie Transformationsprojekte mit OCM allgemein entlang der SAP ACTIVATE Methodologie begleitet werden können. Dabei ist zu beachten, dass kein Transformationsprojekt exakt wie ein anderes abläuft und es immer individuelle Betrachtungen geben sollte, um einen größtmöglichen Mehrwert für das jeweilige Projekt und somit Unternehmen zu erreichen. 

Mit diesem Vorgehen sind die Teilnehmenden gerüstet, um das Erlernte direkt im eigenen Unternehmensalltag einzusetzen.

Was macht das SAP Change Basecamp besonders?

 

Etappe 4: Erinnerungswürdige Reisen werden von anregenden Mitreisenden begleitet

Um den Hands-on Charakter des Formates zu leben, findet das Basecamp im Schulungszentrum der SAP in Walldorf statt. So können die Teilnehmenden ihren Arbeitsalltag verlassen und Personen aus unterschiedlichsten Unternehmen und Branchen kennenlernen. Die Einblicke in die verschiedenen Zeitpunkte und Set-up von Transformationsprojekten, Erfahrungen mit Herausforderungen und Widerständen kommen zur Sprache und bereichern den Workshop jedes Mal auf ein Neues. Alle Teilnehmenden sind somit gemeinsam auf Erkundungstour und das Engagement sowie das Interesse für das Thema OCM spürbar.

Bei dem Basecamp steht das gemeinsame Lernen über Change Management und der Spaß am Entdecken im Mittelpunkt.

Ein Highlight für die Moderator:innen entlang der Reiseroute sind die positiven Rückmeldungen der Teilnehmenden, die nun für personenbezogenen Situationen und Wetterlagen im SAP Projekt besser gewappnet sind.

Wir haben bereits mehrfach die Möglichkeit gehabt das SAP Change Basecamp durchzuführen und Teilnehmenden zu SAP Cloud Change Management Champions (Link zum Badge) auszubilden.

Bisher sind mit uns bereits Personen aus dem Bereich Personalmanagement gereist, die ihren Arbeitsalltag noch holistischer angehen wollten. Weiterhin haben uns Change Berater:innen begleitet, die ihre Fähigkeiten spezifisch für SAP Projekte weiter ausbauen wollten. Auch Projektmanager:innen, die SAP-Transformationen leiten und ihre Fähigkeiten im Bereich OCM ausbauen möchten, waren dabei.

Wann geht die nächste Reise los? 

 

Etappe 5: Nach der Reise ist vor der Reise!

Die nächsten Reisen für das SAP Change Basecamp stehen bereits fest und wir gehen auf Erkundung am:  

 

9. – 10. Oktober 2024

und

27. – 28. November 2024.

 

Du hast Interesse Teil der nächsten Reisegruppe zu sein?
Dann besuch gerne die Anmeldewebsite zum SAP Change Basecamp.
Wir freuen uns auf dich!

Neben den bestehenden Terminen und den stetigen Weiterentwicklungen wird zukünftig auch die Möglichkeit bestehen eine feste Reisegruppe anzumelden, um kundenspezifische Trainings anzubieten. Ab sechs Personen ist dies daher auch vor Ort beim Kunden möglich.

Und auch das SAP Change Basecamp selbst wird im kommenden Jahr neue Orte entdecken, da das Format neben dem Schulungszentrum in Walldorf perspektivisch an weiteren Orten stattfinden wird.

Unabhängig von den neuen Entwicklungen und Orten, die wir erkunden, bleibt unser Ziel stets dasselbe: eine inspirierende und aufregende Reise anbieten, um das Wissen und die Fähigkeiten aller Teilnehmenden zum Thema Change Management zu erweitern.

Und jetzt: Auf zur nächsten Reise… 🙂

entry.title: Re: Cannot delete untransported Business Configuration custom entry, please help
entry.content:
Hello @Attila_Jakosa, Deletion of customer specific entries is not supported by all SSCUI's . Please see accelerator Configuration apps  where SSCUI 105357 Maintain Legal Forms is marked with " Deletion not supported". You should open a customer case to the relevant support component for the area AP-MD-BF-SYN to check and review the duplicate entries in the system as table keys should not allow the creation of duplicate values with the same ID's

Regards,

Lucian

entry.title: Re: Size Limit For Exchange Property in Content Modifier...
entry.content:
Hi ,

In SAP CPI, there is no officially documented limit for the size of properties in the Content Modifier. However, it is generally recommended to keep property sizes small (typically a few kilobytes). Storing large amounts of data (like 50MB or 100MB) in properties is not advisable, as it can lead to performance degradation and potential memory issues.

Regards ,

entry.title: SAP on a Mini PC - my experience with the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial
entry.content:
I have been using the free trial version of the SAP application on a mini PC for about a year now and am very happy with the product. It seems to me that the product is so fine that it is worth promoting and not everyone knows about it yet. I think I can share my insights and experiences with you.


Let's start with what system we are talking about, I need to say that I have a slight problem with this because over the years the product kept changing names. Of course, I must also admit that the software itself and the way it is distributed has also undergone an evolution.

The product in discussion is SAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022. But don't let the name fool you, because you don't need the cloud at all to run it. It is just one of the options. The current version of SAP's free trial is the direct successor to a distribution called SAP ABAP Platform Trial. This one, in turn, evolved from earlier versions - SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP Developer Edition.

ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022

ABAP Cloud Developer Trial is a free platform to try out the ABAP language and toolkit. In reality, we get much more - an SAP system without business logic, but with a whole bunch of extras. So we have the same engine that powers S/4HANA 2022, with working access via SAP-GUI, browser or even from Eclipse. We can use Fiori, at least at the technical layer, because of course the lack of business modules somewhat limits applications. We can also try abapGit, which is configured out of the box, without any problems. In the system image, we will also find SAP Cloud Connector, with which we can easily connect MiniSAP to the BTP platform. It's worth knowing that BTP also has free trial accounts - so we have a great duo.

The software is distributed by sap as a docker container available through DockerHub. This is a very convenient solution supporting ease of installation. So the system itself can be installed in a few minutes, at most half an hour if you have a fast enough connection.

I, referring to my system, often say S/4Hanna-like sandbox. However, I will emphasize once again that this system does not contain business modules. Therefore, it is certainly not for everyone. On the other hand, it has very many applications.

Who is the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial for?

One would like to say that it is for everyone, but in fact it is not for everyone. In my opinion, Developers and Basis Administrators can benefit most from it. And while the former are somewhat limited by the lack of business logic included in the system. While for Basis administrators it is basically an ideal system. And I don't mean at all the meme that without applications and users the system works better. I, as a Basis consultant, am able to test many things on this system: build prototypes of simple solutions, test planned changes, try out features that are not available on older customer systems.

Besides, while running an educational youtube channel about SAP Basis, security and AI, I was able to prepare a lot of material based on this system. If you don't believe, see at least my videos on the 100 most important Basis transactions, SSO configuration using certificates, work processes in SAP or even ICM. Most of them could not have been created if I did not have access to a clean system without client data.

Developers, on the other hand, will also find something for themselves. After all, building ABAP prototypes is not always related to Business Modules. The system provides an excellent opportunity to test new programming techniques, integrations or conduct experiments that would be impossible to do on a real system, even on a client sandbox.

Mini PC

I've been using trial systems from SAP for a year or so on Mini PCs. This is a great solution because the current Mini PCs are very energy efficient and cheap at the same time. You can buy a PC with 16GB RAM, a fast SSD and an Intel n100 processor for around $100. It doesn't offer top-notch performance, but it delivers a great power consumption-to-performance ratio. And even though the Cloud Developer Trial runs on HANA, the performance is perfectly sufficient for one person. If you have any doubts, watch the videos on my channel and see how the system works.

My previous sandbox based on ABAP Platform Trial, which I ran on a Firebat T8 pro PC, cost me $130 in 2023. I recently moved to the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial, switching the MiniPC to Firebat AK2 Plus at the same time. I chose this computer because it allows easy memory expansion, which I did.

If you are curious about what the migration process looked like I encourage you to watch the video. There it shows both securing the data on the previous sandbox, expanding the memory, and inastalling the new system.

Summary

I realize that an intuitive approach to the expensive world of SAP, and in particular to an in-memory database like HANA, evokes associations with very expensive hardware. At first almost everyone asked about SAP systems has connotations of large system requirements. On the other hand, contrary to appearances, current computers are really powerful and the system itself is quite well optimized. As you will see on your own, a cheap mini PC is quite sufficient for operation. I highly encourage you to install and experiment.

entry.title: Re: How to get the actual attachment name from Outbound p...
entry.content:
Hi ,

Please try with this : 

message.setProperty("attachmentName", attachment.getDataSource().getName());

def attachment = message.getProperty("yourAttachmentProperty") --> Replace with your actual attachment property name
def attachmentName = attachment.getDataSource().getName() --> This should fetch the correct attachment name
message.setProperty("attachmentName", attachmentName)

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL=error (STDOUT, STG)# in cloud application programming model in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Unfortunately, the provided logs do not offer enough information to accurately diagnose the issue you are facing during the CAP project deployment.

Please enable and share the DMOL (Deployment Manager Operation Log)

Retrieve Last Deployment IDs:

cf mta-ops --all

Download Detailed Logs (DMOL):

cf dmol -i <operation-id>

entry.title: Re: Put method call on external API ECC6
entry.content:
Hi ,

Please try this : 

DATA: lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
lv_url TYPE string,
lv_response TYPE string,
lv_json TYPE string.

lv_url = 'https://your-api-endpoint.com/assets/' && lv_asset_code.
CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
EXPORTING
url = lv_url
IMPORTING
client = lo_http_client.

lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( 'Content-Type', 'application/json' ).
lv_json = '{"asset_code": "' && lv_asset_code && '"}'.
lo_http_client->request->set_method( 'PUT' ).
lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( lv_json ).

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->send
RECEIVING
response = lv_response.

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: Ad-hoc Service with Fixed Price billing or Billing Plan
entry.content:
hi Patrick

Thanks for your question. Defining the complete billing plan, including the billing dates is not supported in Quotation. Mainly because at the time of quote, you don't know when the quote will be accepted and when the first billing should be done. 

However, in release 2023 FPS2 we plan to deliver the functionality of billing plan template. This is a predefined template that can be selected in the service order and then the billing lines will be created automatically. For the billing dates we will use date types and date rules in the billing plan template. It will also be possible to already select the billing plan template in the quote. The billing plan itself will only be created in the order, but at least the information (description, long description) of the billing plan template can be included in the quote output. 

Regards, Gert

entry.title: Re: Recommended implementation of a dependent branch abro...
entry.content:
Hello Avnish,

thanks for you answere. But on the SAP help Portal there is another Recommendatioan:

Plants Abroad

Recommendation

You should only use this function if you have several warehouses, distribution centers, or plants abroad.

If you only have individual warehouses abroad, or similar, it is better to have a separate company code set up for each warehouse, for example. Otherwise, for example, a single sales office would manage the function and scope of the Customizing settings for plants abroad in a disproportionally complex manner.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration Looping Process Call
entry.content:
Hi @DenizZilyas 

Thank you for the wonderful blog. I'm currently trying it now for a PoC to retrieve BOM data from S4.

For the first loop we are successfully getting 100 records but in the second loop, error is com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: HTTP Request failed with error : Target host is null, cause: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Target host is null

Looking into the logs, our first loop has the correct REQUEST_URL: GET http://serverurl/BOMHeader?$expand=to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem&$select=BOMHeaderText,TechnicalObject,to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem/BillOfMaterialComponent,to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem/BillOfMaterialItemQuantity,to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem/IdentifierBOMItem

However our second loop does not have the correct server URL: GET BOMHeader?$expand=to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem&$select=BOMHeaderText,TechnicalObject,to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem/BillOfMaterialComponent,to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem/BillOfMaterialItemQuantity,to_MaintBillOfMaterialItem/IdentifierBOMItem&$skiptoken=100 HTTP/1.1

We also cannot find __next in the response of the first loop. What we can only see is below in the property.

 

For your expert advice if I'm missing anything here or this is a possible limitation in the S4 pagination. Thank you and appreciate your help here.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
I would like to show-off my keyboard setup, since I care a lot about keyboard ergonomics. The picture shows a split keyboard, mounted on two upside-down laptop mounts, such that it is positioned left and right to your lap, in an angle of perhaps 75 degrees. This is an extremely comfortable typing position (but you need to know your keyboard layout by hard). Note the black mini touch pad I mounted just above the right-hand keyboard, so I only rarely have to reach for the touch pad in the middle. If you use your mouse a lot, this is not for you.

I do almost everything with the keyboard alone. The (Mac) software tools I use for this purpose are homerow and Better Touch Tool (BTT). With BTT, I define shortcuts that can bring to front each app that I use on a day-to-day basis. This way, I don't use virtual desktops, since I can address every app directly. To understand how homerow works, visit their site. (In short, homerow is for the desktop what vimium is for the browser.)

These SW tools are only available for Mac, but vimium is available as browser plugin on every operating system.

entry.title: Speech To Text in SAP Mobile Start on iOS
entry.content:
Hi there,

I'm working on an CAP & SAPUI5 APP which leverages Speech Recognition. To reduce the dependence on fast internet, we decided to use the built-in speech to text functions.

To achieve Support across all Browsers we use either webkitSpeechRecognition or SpeechRecoginition depending on which is available. This appears to be working fine on Chrome, Edge and Safari(even Mobile) and on SAP Mobile Start for Android

      onSpeechRecognition() {
        let oSpeechRecognition;
        if ('SpeechRecognition' in window || 'webkitSpeechRecognition' in window) {
          oSpeechRecognition = new webkitSpeechRecognition() || new SpeechRecognition();
        } else {
          MessageBox.error('SpeechRecognition is not supported on this Device');
        }
        this.setControls('isRecording', true);

        oSpeechRecognition.continuous = true;
        oSpeechRecognition.lang = navigator.language || 'ch-DE';

        oSpeechRecognition.onresult = function (oSpeechResult) {
          let sInput = '';
          for (let oResult of oSpeechResult.results) {
            sInput += oResult[0].transcript;
          }
          this.byId('message').setValue(sInput);
        }.bind(this);
        oSpeechRecognition.start();
        this.stt = oSpeechRecognition;
      },

But when opening the same App in Mobile Start for iOS, the SpeechRecognition doesn't work. The prompt for asking for Microphone permissions isn't even shown.

This surprises me especially since the Blog about Mobile Start from @ThiloBerndt mentions Speech-To-Text explicitly.

Any help and advice would be appreciated.

Best regards, Michael

entry.title: Shipment number: To set it to external numbering and automatically assign it like internal numbering
entry.content:
皆さん、こんにちは

現在、出荷登録時(T-cd:VT01N)に番号範囲を外部番号に設定し、内部番号のように自動的に番号が割り当てられるシステムの開発を検討しています。

 

そこで、専門家の皆様に以下の質問をさせていただきたいと思います。

 

(1)貨物を作成する際、番号を捕捉する出口はありますか?

(2)「IF_EX_BADI_LE_SHIPMENT~AT_SAVE」という方法を考えています。

ただし、「MV56AF99N_FCODE_ROUTINES」を含めるの「外部出荷番号の確認」が先に移動し、エラーが発生します。

理想的には、「IF_EX_BADI_LE_SHIPMENT~AT_SAVE」という方法で自動番号付けをしたいと思います。

 

<理想的な例>
外線番号範囲「A0001~A9999」
は、以下の画面で出荷番号を変更せずに保存してください。

(3)
「A0001」「A0002」などには、出荷番号が自動的に付与されます。(自動採番の回数はアドオンテーブル等で管理されることを前提としています)

 

よろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Enabling Broadcast Features and Root cause Analysis for Broadcasts Failure in SAP C4C
entry.content:
Hey All,

I am writing this blog to serve as central information on how to make use of Broadcast Bursting and User Specific features while creating Broadcasts for Reports or Dashboards and the Root cause analysis for Broadcast Failure. Here you will find the steps to achieve in your system and get to know about how they work technically.

Introduction

  • I hope you are aware that we can create broadcasts for Reports and Dashboards.
  • Defining the recipients who are going to receive the broadcasts is important, and it is mandatory while creating the broadcasts.
  • There are two ways available to determine the recipients. 
    • Broadcast Bursting.
    • User Specific.

First, let us see the broadcast bursting feature. 

  • Bursting feature allows you to broadcast reports to multiple recipients by e-mail.

Prerequisite

  •  We have to maintain the addresses of the recipients in the master data, so that we can select a characteristic in which the e-mail addresses are maintained. The system can determine the recipients from this information.

Getting Started

  • As you can see from the below screenshot, I have created new Report (UserEmployeeLogon) and I have added E-Mail standalone field, and it shows the E- mail address.

Fig 1. Report View

  • let's create the new Broadcast for this report as shown below.

Fig 2. Broadcast Creation

  • Select the checkbox Use Broadcast Bursting, the section to add e-mail addresses of the recipients is hidden.
  • For the new field Attribute for Email, select a characteristic, in which you have maintained the email address. Now, the report will be automatically sent to all the relevant recipients, without you having to add each individual e-mail addresses separately.
  • Unlike a normal broadcast, entire data is not sent to all recipients. The report data is filtered for each recipient based on their e-mail addresses, which is identified by the values of the attribute you select. This ensures that every recipient only receives the data filtered for their e-mail address.Fig 3. Adding the field attributeFig 4. Broadcast Creation Details
  • Click on Save and CloseFig 5. Broadcast List screen
  • Click on Start Now button as shown below to start the broadcast.
  • You can see the status under the Last Run column (yellow indicates broadcast is in progress and green indicates successful run).Fig 6. Start Broadcast
  • Fig 7. Broadcast Runs Status
  • Broadcast email sent to user as shown below.Fig 8. Broadcast E-mail received

Now let's see the Broadcasting by User Specific Data.

  • We can decide if the recipients are to receive data according to their authorizations. To do this, select the User specific checkbox.
  • This field is visible only when the object type is a report, and the recipient is a Business User in the system.Fig 9. User Specific Feature
  • Broadcast email sent to user as shown below.
  • Fig 10. Broadcast E-mail recieved
  • Note that when the checkbox is unselected, the data is broadcast using the authorizations of the user who creates the broadcast. Ensure that the user has access rights to the work center views to which the report is assigned in order to start the report.

Note: - If you use the broadcast bursting feature, the system cannot check if the recipients have a valid user. For this reason, it is not possible to use the User specific and the broadcast bursting feature together - please either use the broadcast bursting or make the broadcast user specific (after selecting users as recipients).

Now let us see the reasons for Root cause analysis for Broadcasts failures.

  1. If the creator of the broadcasts is locked or inactive, then the broadcast will not run, so make sure that the creator of the broadcast is active and unlocked or create a new broadcast.
  2. The creator of the broadcast must have access to the report involved and also make sure that the user can see the report in the analysis work center.
  3. The user who created broadcast doesn't have access to the Workcenter view that the report is assigned to so check into the Access Rights of the user who created Broadcast and see if they have access to the Workcenter view that the report (Broadcasted) is assigned to.
  4. Multiple broadcasts scheduled at the same time: Check how many broadcasts scheduled at the same time run in background jobs (it's advised to schedule broadcast runs one by one with a time difference of at least 60 minutes).

Kindly note that all these information gathered from SAP Help documentations/KBA's.

Conclusion: - With this note, I’d like to conclude my blogpost on Enabling Broadcast Features and Root cause Analysis for Broadcasts Failure in SAP C4C.

I hope the information provided in this blog post was useful to you. In case if you are looking for more details on this topic you can find it on SAP documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/sap-cloud-for-customer-analytics-guide/broadcasts . Please feel free to provide your feedback or ask question in the comments section and I’ll be glad to respond to you. Happy Learning!

Regards,
Sudarshan

entry.title: Error message no. AIST009: Profit Center change in Investment order and Asset Under Construction.
entry.content:
In this blog I would like to explain how to change Profit Center in Investment order and Asset Under Construction. Error message no. AIST009:
Please note: In my case Profit center was not assigned to any of the cost center master data. Profit center was deriving from custom table based on Cost center But same case might useful when you change PC assignment in Cost center master data. This part I have not tested.


Here is an example of Investment Order and AUC master data.

When we try to change profit center in Asset master data in AS02:


When we try to change profit center in Investment Order master data in KO02:

Usually, Asset Under Construction is created with reference to Investment Order. As both have linked each other we cannot change profit centre individually at AUC or IO level.


Here the solution:
Step 1: Go to KO02 transaction, Change the profit center, then click on Extra and select Asset Under Construction as below.

Step 2: Then it will open AUC with edit mode. Here you change the profit center and press enter. You will get a pop up. Select the option as per your requirement. (Here I selected Yes.) and then press back or F3 and save the Investment Order.

Step 3: After saving the Investment Order, Profit Center gets updated in both IO and AUC. Below picture shows result of Profit center changed.

You might face some other issue depending on your system configuration.
Please feel free to ask addition questions if any or correct me if I am wrong.
Thank you!

SAP S/4HANA Finance

entry.title: Fixing Interview Scheduling Errors in SAP SuccessFactors with Outlook Integration
entry.content:
Scheduling interviews is an important part of a recruiter’s job. With SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM), you can make this easier by connecting it to Microsoft Outlook Calendar. But sometimes things go wrong, and you might face errors that interrupt interview scheduling. This blog explains how to troubleshoot these issues and restore your Outlook integration for smooth interview planning.

Common Error: Expired Outlook Integration Details

One frequent problem is expired Outlook integration details. If you try to schedule an interview and get an error like "Invalid client ID," it usually means the credentials (Client ID, Client Secret, Tenant ID) have expired. This stops the integration from accessing interviewers’ availability in Outlook.

Steps to Fix the Issue:

  1. Go to the Setup Page: Navigate to the Interview Scheduling Outlook Integration settings in your SAP SuccessFactors system.
  2. Check for Expired Credentials: Look at the Client ID, Client Secret, Tenant ID, and Email ID. If any of these are expired, you’ll see an error message telling you they need to be updated.
  3. Contact IT Support: If credentials have expired, get in touch with IT team. They will update the details.

Verify the Integration: Once IT updates the credentials, click the "Verify" button. If the issue is resolved, the credentials will turn green, showing that the integration is active.

Scheduling Interviews with Outlook:
Once Outlook Integration is enabled, you can easily schedule interviews using updated information about interviewers' availability from their Outlook Calendar. The system automatically refreshes every hour, so only available times are shown in the scheduling grid.

Benefits of Outlook Integration:

Real-Time Availability: Interviewers' schedules are synced with Microsoft Outlook Calendar, showing their latest availability.

  • Fewer Conflicts: Only available slots are shown, reducing the chance of scheduling conflicts.
  • Efficient Process: The interview scheduling system is read-only, so no changes are made directly to Outlook, but all data stays updated.

By ensuring the integration is always up to date, recruiters can avoid errors and schedule interviews smoothly.

entry.title: Creating a Pay Component with monthly amount based on other Pay component with annual amount
entry.content:
Hi there, came with another blog, wherein we got a requirement to populate Pay component 1 (say Annual Salary) with annual amount to a another Pay component 2 (say Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP) with monthly amount.

This requirement came into effect as “Payroll team” want the amount in “Monthly figure” whereas client want the amount to be displayed as “Annual figure” on employee’s profile.

We have met this requirement with business rule and placed this rule as “OnSave” on compensation information section under Manage Business Configuration.

First step is to create both pay components via Manage org job and pay structures.

  • Go to Manage org job and pay structures. Click on create new. Create Annual Salary pay component. Below is the screenshot of the same.

 

 

 

  • Similarly, create another pay component 2 (Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP) with “MON” frequency
  • After creating both pay components, provide respective RBPs.

 

Then we will start creating the business rule. Base object of the rule is “Compensation Information Model”.

The rule says that when Amount is not equal to Null where pay component is Annual salary, then create a new record with pay component as (Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP) where frequency is “MON”.

Amount is calculated with Divide function, where the dividend is “Amount of the Annual Salary” and Divisor is “12”.

 

 

This rule is placed as “On Save”.

So, now when you add “Annual Salary” on employee profile under Compensation section, then automatically “Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP” will be created.

Thank you for reading the blog. I hope this will help you in meeting certain requirements. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are always welcome.  We’d love to hear about your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights you have, so please feel free to share them in the comments section below. Happy Learning

entry.title: Usage of Data Crawler in Condition using BRF+ approach in SAP TM
entry.content:
In this post we are going to understand an interesting topic in TM that cater to various scenarios with use of multiple option parameters.

Condition is a mechanism used to determine dependent values to filter Freight Units, Freight Orders, FUBR, Organizational Units like Purchase Organization & Sales Organization etc.

Usage of BRF+ approach in Condition provides a flexible approach to maintain dependent values with the help of BRF+ decision table in the FIORI.

Data Crawler is a concept used in TM primarily to extract values from different Business Objects and use these values as dependent values in Condition to filter various elements like Freight Units, Freight Orders, FUBR and so on.

We are going to understand the topic by illustrating a simple scenario widely used in SAP TM.

Requirement: To filter Freight Order types based on the combination of Source Location and Freight Unit Type.

Here, dependent values are Source Location and Freight Unit type that are required to be extracted from Business Objects by using Data Crawler approach and then these values are maintained in the BRF+ Decision Table via Condition that will enable the TM system to generate or create Freight Order types based on Source Location & Freight Unit combination via Planning Optimizer engine.

We have divided this post in three sections in sequential order to understand the topic.

First Section: To identify and extract data from required fields using Data crawler approach and set up Data Definition Access in SAP TM system.

Second Section:  Set up parameters in the BRF+ Decision Table using Conditions with identified fields from first section.

Third Section: Test the se ups by stimulating a scenario.

We have taken one sample Freight Order 6100003468 to identify fields from where the dependent values are to be extracted from.

First Section:

Source Location: SP_RTSP. The Technical field name is LOG_LOCID.

Freight Unit Type: RTFU. The Technical Field name is TOR_TYPE.

Next step is to identify these technical fields from the Business Object.

Run the Tcode: /n/BOBT and then move to /SCMTMS/TOR à ROOT.

We locate the Technical field – LOG_LOCID under the node of STOP_FIRST.

Similarly, we have to determine the field for Freight Unit type.

The Freight Unit for this Freight Order 610000348 is 4500001373.

We have to follow the same path of the Business Object and identify the node under which the Freight Unit type is available.

We locate the Technical field – TOR_TYPE under the node of REQ_TOR.

Now, we have to define Data Crawler Profile and Condition type to extract these two values from the identified fields.

In the SAP TM:

SPRO-> IMG-> Transportation Management -> Basic Functions -> Conditions.

Define Data Crawler Profile

For first parameter “Source Location” – we define the Profile: RT_TOR_SOURCE_LOC.

This is updated with BO and Node names with the path that is navigated to reach the technical field of the Source Location.

For second parameter “Freight Unit Type”, we define the Profile: RT_FU.

This is updated with BO and Node names with the path that is to be navigated to reach the technical field of Freight Unit type.

We have now defined the structure to fetch the value from  two technical fields.

These two Data Crawler Profiles are then assigned to Data Access Condition.

Data Access Condition

We created two Data Access Conditions for these two parameters – Source Location and Freight Unit Type.

We have assigned Data Crawler Profile and maintain the Field name with technical field.

Similarly, we have to create Data Access Condition for Freight Unit Type.

We have now completed the section one with the identification and extraction of relevant data for two parameters by defining the structure using Data Crawler Profiles & Data Access Definitions.

Second Section: 

Set up parameters in the BRF+ Decision Table using Data Access Definitions with identified fields from first section by creating Condition in the FIORI tool.

We have created a Condition: RT_MULTI.

Go to Data Access Definition –

We have added two Data Access Definitions for both parameters.

When it is drilled down, we can see Data Crawler Profile and the relevant field.

We now maintain parameters in the Condition FU_MULTI:

Source Location

Freight Unit (FU)

Freight Order

SP_RTSH

RTFU

ARFO

SP_RTSP

RTFU

RTFO

The combination of Source Location and Freight Unit type will determine the required Fright Order type.

Third Section: 

We will test the Condition set up by creating one scenario.

In the Planning Profile, we will set up this Condition in the Capacity Document Creation Setting:

A Sales Order is created with two stages using Incoterms Location Stage building concept.

In the Cockpit, we can see two stages generated for the Freight Unit 4500001417

Freight Unit 4500001417:

The Freight Unit Type is RTFU

Stages in Freight Unit –

For Stage 1: Source Location is SP_RTSP

For Stage 2: Source Location is SP_RTSH

The system will create two Freight Orders for each stage and as per the Condition set up, system will create Freight Orders for the Stage 1 as RTFO type and for Stage 2 as ARFO type respectively.

Freight Order 6100003517 is created for the Stage 1 via Optimizer Planning. The Freight Order type is RTFO.

Stage:

For the Stage 2, Freight Order 6100003518 is created via Optimizer Planning. The Freight Order type is ARFO.

Stage:

I hope this gives a bit insight on Data Crawler approach and the usage of Condition with BRF+ table using it.

Any comments or feedback is appreciated.

Regards

Sourav

entry.title: Delaying Sending Batch Subitems to ERP
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), integration with the ERP system is crucial for seamless operations. The default setting for transferring Batch Subitems is immediately after creation. However, there might be scenarios where there is a need to delay the sending of batch subitems to the ERP system, like when there are long processing QRFC message queues or other similar performance issues. In S4HANA 104, this can be achieved by using implicit enhancement in class /SCWM/CL_ERP_CTRLPROCS_S4. This blog post will guide you through the steps to accomplish this.

Understanding the Context

The class /SCWM/CL_ERP_CTRLPROCS_S4 is responsible for various control processes in the integration between SAP EWM and SAP ERP. One of the control mechanisms is the batch split, which is managed by the field ss_erp_ctrlprocs_default-od_batch_split. By setting the value of this field to 1, you can control the timing of when batch subitems are sent to the ERP system.

Step-by-Step Guide

Step 1: Access the Class /SCWM/CL_ERP_CTRLPROCS_S4

Open your SAP GUI and navigate to transaction code SE24 (Class Builder).

Enter the class name /SCWM/CL_ERP_CTRLPROCS_S4 and click on "Display".

Step 2: Locate the Field ss_erp_ctrlprocs_default-od_batch_split

Once inside the class display, navigate to the "Attributes" tab.

Look for the attribute ss_erp_ctrlprocs_default-od_batch_split. This field controls the behavior of batch split sending.

By default, ss_erp_ctrlprocs_default-od_batch_split’s value is ‘/scwm/if_mapping_constants=>sc_cp_od_batch_spl_immed’ i.e. ‘ ’.

Step 3: Modify the Field Value

To modify the value of this field, you will need to access the relevant method where this field is utilized. Typically, this would be within the methods that handle outbound delivery processes.

Navigate to the "Methods" tab and identify the method where batch subitems are processed. This could be methods related to outbound deliveries or batch processing.

Step 4: Implement the Change

Open the identified method in edit mode and search for enhancement points to implement the enhancement, where based on required condition the value of ss_erp_ctrlprocs_default-od_batch_split can be updated to ‘1’ using SC_CP_OD_BATCH_SPL_AT_GI as constant. Save and activate your changes.

Step 5: Testing

Perform thorough testing to ensure that the batch subitems are now being delayed as expected.

Create and process outbound deliveries in EWM and monitor the integration with ERP to verify the delay in sending batch subitems.

Additional Considerations

Ensure that you have appropriate authorizations to make changes in the system.

Consult with your SAP Basis and development teams to coordinate any changes in a production environment.

Document your changes and communicate with relevant stakeholders.

Conclusion

By setting the field ss_erp_ctrlprocs_default-od_batch_split to 1 in the class /SCWM/CL_ERP_CTRLPROCS_S4, you can effectively delay the sending of batch subitems to the ERP system. This can be particularly useful in scenarios where immediate batch processing is not required or needs to be controlled. Always ensure to follow best practices for testing and change management when implementing such modifications.

entry.title: Cancel Picking of EWM Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Cancel Picking:

Cancel picking refers to the process of undoing action that was previously recorded. This action is necessary when an error occurs during the picking process, such as picking the wrong items or quantities, or when a picking task needs to be retracted for other reasons.


Steps to perform Goods Issue Reversal, Cancel Picking and Outbound Delivery Zero Out Quantity:

Step1: Display Outbound delivery using transaction code /SCWM/PRDO

 

 

Step2: Navigate to the Goods issue option and select the Reverse goods issue option from the drop down menu

 

 

Reverse goods issue is completed.

Then click on the Outbound delivery option and delete the ODO and save.

 

 

 

 

Step3: Cancellation of picking using the transaction code /N/SCWM/CANCPICK

In the find option select the Document number and provide the outbound delivery number.

 

Select the document number line and then select the “Release Stock + WT in foreground”.

 

Click on the form view

 

In the next screen provide the following details

  1. a) Quantity
  2. b) Destination bin 
  3. c) Warehouse process type

Check mark the option Confirm WT and then click on create and save.

 

 

 

The warehouse order is created.

 

Step4: Confirm the warehouse order using the transaction code /N/SCWM/TO_CONF

Provide the warehouse order number and then click on Confirm+Save option.

 

 

Step5: Display the Outbound delivery using the transaction code /N/SCWM/PRDO

 

 

Navigate to the Process code option and then select the option “Adjust Delivery Quantity” from the drop-down option.

 

 

Select the Process code “O001”

 

 

The quantity is changed to Zero.

 

 

Then click on the Create outbound delivery option.

 

 

 

 

Conclusion:

Goods Issue Reversal, Cancel Picking and Outbound Delivery Zero Out Quantity provided.

entry.title: Error Message while creating Pick HU - /SCWM/HUGENERAL 099
entry.content:
During  Pick HU creation in EWM - The error message "Requested content cannot be executed - Please contact your system administrator" is observed.

One of the root cause for this error message is the NR status of the HU number range. Below is the scenario and resolution for this issue.

The internal HU number range interval is setup for the warehouse, while NR status is 1389 as per the below screenshot.

Though Last HU# 2000 is created for this warehouse , NR status is not updated to 2000. Due to which we are observing error message  "Requested content cannot be executed - Please contact your system administrator" - /SCWM/HUGENERAL - 099

Resolution : Modify NR status to update with the latest HU# created in the EWM warehouse. Error message /SCWM/HUGENERAL 099 does not provide more specifics about the exact root cause issue. This blog explains about one of the root cause and resolution for this error message.

entry.title: Procedure For End-To-End Encryption And Signing Of File Through SFTP Via Integration Suite PART-1
entry.content:
I have a requirement where I must process a payment file generated in SAP through integration suite by encrypting and signing the file, sending the file to the bank server. We are sending and receiving the payment file through SFTP, to connect to the bank server, we have provided them with a PUBLIC SSH KEY. We were given the public key of the receiver, folder path to place the file, server address and credentials of the same to login and we must process the file generated in SAP to the receiver by encrypting and signing the file. We should encrypt the data using receiver’s public key and sign the same using our private key.

Procedure: -

I am providing a glimpse of usage of the SSH keys and public and private keys of both tenant and receiver below, SSH PRIVATE KEY of the tenant is used to connect the bank server, we share our SSH PUBLIC KEY to the receiver who is bank in our case so, they will map the SSH PUBLIC KEY in their system and provide us a user assigned to the SSH PUBLIC KEY. BANK PUBLIC KEY is provided to encrypt the payment file in CPI/INTEGRATION SUITE, TENANT PUBLIC KEY is given to the bank to verify the signature of the tenant where it is signed by the TENANT’s PRIVATE KEY in CPI/INTEGRATION SUITE. Lastly BANK’s PRIVATE KEY is used to decrypt the file when they receive the file from CPI through SFTP and they will verify the same using TENANT’s PUBLIC KEY. We must add the public key of the receiver and the public key and private key of ours generated through the procedure explained below to PGP keys in monitor -> manage security -> PGP Keys. Since this is a huge procedure, I will be sharing all the information related in two blog posts.

PROCEDURE TO GENERATE AND CONVERT PRIVATE PPK FORMAT KEY TO OPEN SSH KEY PEM FORMAT AND .P12 FORMAT THROUGH PUTTY KEY GENERATOR AND OPENSSL

How to generate a public and private SSH key using putty key generator (which will be used for authentication): -

Above picture shows the main screen of putty key generator

 

Steps to generate the SSH public and private keys: -

There is an option of generate and load at the bottom right of the screen shown above, click on generate and you will be getting below screen,

The above screen shows the generation of the public and private keys, just move the cursor as instructed above and the keys will be generated.

This is the screen showing the creation of the keys, just click on save public key and provide a name and location, the public key is saved, then click on the save private key by providing key passphrase and confirm the same and save, now you have successfully generated public and private keys using a putty key generator, public key is saved in file format and private key is saved in PPK format, but in order to save the keys in SAP CPI we need the file in .p12 format so we need to convert the PPK format file to .p12 format and for that we need OPENSSL.

 Converting the file from PPK to .p12:-

Here is the link to download OPENSSL for your system https://slproweb.com/products/Win32OpenSSL.html, using this download the OPENSSL and place private key which in PPK format in the bin folder of the OPENSSL, open command prompt in windows as an administrator and provide file path to the bin folder as a command, access dir of the bin folder and you should be able to see the private key file in PPK format you pasted in OPENSSL bin folder, https://www.kellton.com/kellton-tech-blog/how-to-generate-import-ssh-keys-in-sap-pi/po is the link with procedure explanation, using the following command openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key <PrivateKey.pem> -out <x509_PrivateKey.pem> ,using the above command, you will be able to convert the file from PPK format to x509 PEM format, using the following command  openssl pkcs12 -export -in <x509_PrivateKey.pem> -inkey <PrivateKey.pem> -out <PrivateKey.p12>, using the above command, you will be able to convert the file from PEM x509 format to .p12 format which can be uploaded to SAP CPI keystore as a keypair.

Procedure for generating key pair (includes both public and private keys):

We need gpg4win to generate keys, we can find the required version to download and install by using the link here  https://files.gpg4win.org/gpg4win-2.3.4.exe, we require 2.3.4 version of gpg4win, after downloading the same please make sure to deselect the Kleopatra and select GPA while installing the components in the package, after installation procedure is completed, please create tenant specific directories and make sure to have three files specified gpa.conf, gpg.conf, run_gpa.bat in the same directory for key management, this procedure of creating tenant specific directories and placing the above files in the directory is mandatory for each tenant separately, find the link to download those file here Creating Tenant-Specific File Directories | SAP Help Portal, adapt the file run_gpa.bat by entering the path to the tenant-specific directory. These files are required to configure the usage of the GPA tool. The file run_gpa.bat sets the shell variable GNUPGHOME to the tenant-specific directory. The files gpa.conf and gpg.conf contain configurations for GPA and GPG. The file gpg.conf, for example, determines the strength of the applied encryption.

After running the GPA tool, you will be seeing a screen like this,

On the top you can find Windows, on clicking it you can find keyring manager, just click it and setup the keyring manager.

After setup you can find the screen like below,

Please go the keys in the top; after clicking the keys you can find an option of new key after clicking the new key you will be getting a popup like this,

Complete the procedure by filling up your name, mail id and passphrase and select the folder path where you want to save the keys, and you are now able to generate the keys. After the keys are generated, please find that the public and private keys both are saved in a single file, you can just copy and paste both in separate files through notepad/notepad++ and just upload the same in the PGP keys in manage security, monitor.

Procedure to consume the KEYS in to the PGP KEYS:

Please upload the keys generated through GPA to the PGP Keys in manage security below,

Go to PGP keys and there you can find an option of add like below,

Please go to add and there you can find public keys and secret keys options, upload public and secret keys separately and later you can observe both the keys will be in one type when you uploaded both public and secret keys, if not it shows only public or secret type in type, please find below reference image,

Till now, we have seen generating SSH keys and public private keys, we will see iflow design part in the next blog post.

 

Please let me know any points/suggestions regarding this blog post, so I will address the same in next blog post. We value your feedback.

entry.title: Use SOAP API with ProcessDirect to call a RFC
entry.content:
In this article, I am going to document how I achieve SOAP based integration. let me give a short background we have more than 30 BAPIs  which are consumed by Non-SAP applications. The simplest way is to create 30 SOAP APIs/iFlows which call request reply and call a RFC adapter in the end.

So, I asked myself, can I do it more smartly, that will help in future as well as minimize my current workload also. Then I figure out two options:

  1. Create an iFlow which can be invoked from another iFlow (SOAP API in this example) and that execute the RFC and give response back to the calling iFlow.
  2. Create a SOAP API Proxy in SAP APIM, against each BAPI and call a standard SOAP API from Cloud Integration which calls the RFC functional module. 

In this blog I am documenting Option 1 but I think Option 2 is more smart, the only issue I find in implementing Option 2 is, I was facing issue to verify the SOAP WSDL definition in proxy. Due to which I opted Option 1 in the end. But to avoid direct access I create proxy for each project in SAP APIM and expose the proxy endpoint to the end user. 

so, let's begin with option 1 today. In my next blog, I document option 2 as well, might it will be helpful to someone.  

Steps 1: Create an iFlow 

  • Create (or Edit) a package named "General"
  • After, create a new iFlow (Artifact type "Integration Flow") named it "Call RFC"
  • Connect Sender with Start event using adapter "ProcessDirect" from the list. Go to settings of ProcessDirect and then set an address in the connection tab (this address will be used later, so copy it in the notepad for later use).

  • Then, add Request-Reply and connect an arrow with Receiver using RFC adapter from the list, and one is already connected with end event (leave it as it is). Open the setting and set the destination of RFC.

  • Save and Deploy the artifact.  

Step 2: Create a SOAP API 

  • Create a new artifact with type API ( choose SOAP API on the next screen) in the existing package or create new one.
  • Remove the existing "Handle Request" integration sub-process along with Process Call  Step. 
  • Configure the SOAP adapter setting 
SOAP Connection Details 
Addressas required (/salesforce/get-customer-details)
Service DefinitionWSDL
URL to WSDLbrowse or select the appropriate WSDL definition

  • Connect end event with receiver and select ProcessDirect as adapter from list. Open the setting and set the address (you have copied in the notepad in step 1). 

  • Save and Deploy the artifact.

Step 3: Test the API

  • Go to monitor > Integrations and APIs and the search the newly deployed SOAP API

  • Download the WSDL definition
  • Open the SoapUI and create a new SOAP Project (go to file menu)
  • Give a proper name and browse the wsdl file you downloaded earlier. 
  • A new request will be created in the project head. 
  • Input the username/password in authorization tab under basic authentication.
  • and submit the request, upon successful connectivity you will get the desired result. 

 

Note: Then I simply create SOAP APIs and update the WSDL definition accordingly.

entry.title: Guide to use HANA NSE in the BW/4HANA system
entry.content:
It is critical for the HANA systems not to overload the memory, because loads/unloads are affecting the performance drastically.

We are constantly working on reducing the memory footprint to improve the performance and avoid buying additional memory since that could be complicated by various reasons like hardware costs, unavailability of particular memory planks in the market, a necessity to reconfigure the current landscape etc.

So, in addition to archiving, we decided also to implement a HANA NSE.

Native storage extension is quite a flexible tool to reduce the data memory footprint, which is not so complicated to set up.

 

NSE makes much sense e.g. in the SAP BW/4HANA environment, since you usually have quite a lot of data, plus it supports a scale-out scenario, the only thing you need to know, is that HANA does not consider the NSE table for the redistributions, so you need to take care of them manually if needed.

 

 

You can put to the NSE tables, table Indexes, table partitions, or single table columns, they will be kept in the storage, and only the required pages will be loaded, not the whole table/column.

This has an effect also on the storage sizing, the page loadable objects require more storage space and also will be a bit slower for the querying, so this should be taken into account

 

 

There could be several ways to identify it, there is a recommended way from SAP to run the NSE Advisor, but as I know some customers do not want to create additional load on HANA with the statistics collection, so they do it with the commonsense analysis, e.g. checking the EWA for the biggest tables which is not accessed too much, or put to the NSE the data which is not needed to be queried/reloaded often, e.g. the data for the previous years in the reporting which needs

 

In our experience, we did not find a significant increase in the HANA load with the Advisor activated, so we decided to go with the NSE advisor

 

You cannot run NSE load unit conversion DDLs on the following types of SAP HANA system tables:

Tables that were created on SAP HANA versions 2.0 SPS 00 or earlier, and never rewritten or merged into later/higher SAP HANA versions.

In our scenario we used HANA 2.0 SP5, so we followed the note 2927591, but for the different SPs you need to follow the different notes

 

 

So first of all, you need to convert the tables into the HANA 2.0 format, in our case there were ~1000 tables with the old format, and the conversion took around 2 hours. There were no problems in the normal BW/HANA operations, only many alerts appeared during the conversions of these tables, but you can ignore them. In general, you can test this process in the quality system to ensure there are no problems during the conversions

 

Before that, you need to find all of them, by running the following select:

 

select * from M_CS_COLUMNS_PERSISTENCE where PERSISTENCE_TYPE = 'VIRTUAL_FILE' or PERSISTENCE_TYPE = 'VIRTUAL_PAGED'

 

It also makes sense to run the delta merge for the tables to be converted:

 

select count(*) from <table_name>

merge delta of <table_name> force rebuild

 

 

 

Download the stored procedure for your SP from the relevant SAP note, e.g. for HANA 2.0 SP7 you would need SAP Note 3320095

 

After you create the procedure in HANA, you need to execute the following:

CALL PREPARE_PAGE_LOADABLE_PERSISTENCE('<schema_name>', ?)

 

 

After the procedure successfully executed you need to start the NSE advisor, and let it run for quite some time, depends on your scenario. E.g. if you have only the monthly reports you cannot do any analysis before the whole month passed, the data would not be relevant 

 

Start NSE_ADVISOR:

ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('indexserver.ini','system') SET ('cs_access_statistics','collection_enabled') = 'true' WITH RECONFIGURE;

 

After you have collected all the necessary statistics you can disable it:

ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('indexserver.ini','system') SET

('cs_access_statistics','collection_enabled') = 'false'

WITH RECONFIGURE;

 

In our scenario we did not disable that, so it keeps running, and we have new inputs every 2-3 months for the objects which we could put into NSE

 

 

The $trexexternalkey$ values stand for the indexes, which HANA recommend to put into the NSE, since they are not being accessible too often.

 

Putting the data into NSE:

You can run it for the table:

alter table <schema_name>.<table_name>_BOOKM page loadable

for the column:

alter table <schema_name>."/BI0/SCO_ITEM_NO" alter ("SID" ALTER PAGE LOADABLE)

for putting the index of the table to NSE you need at first finding the name of it

SELECT SCHEMA_NAME, TABLE_NAME, INDEX_NAME, INDEX_TYPE, CONSTRAINT FROM INDEXES WHERE TABLE_NAME = <table_name>;

 

After finding the index name:

 

ALTER INDEX <schema_name>."_SYS_TREE_CS_#1816272_#0_#P0" PAGE LOADABLE;

 

 

to get it back to the default behavior you need to run the following command:

ALTER TABLE "<table_name>" DEFAULT LOADABLE CASCADE;

ALTER INDEX <schema_name>."_SYS_TREE_CS_#1816272_#0_#P0" default LOADABLE;

 

 

To check the tables in the NSE you can run the following command:

select * from tables where load_unit = 'PAGE'

To check the indexes in the NSE you can run the following command:

select * from indexes where load_unit = 'PAGE'

To check the columns in the NSE you can run the following command:

select * from m_cs_all_columns where load_unit = 'PAGE'

It's also important to know, how much memory you saves by putting the data into the NSE, and here are 2 ways of doing it

Usually, in the NSE Advisor, you see the amount of memory you will save putting the table to the NSE, with summing up the memory size in the main memory columns
 
select * from Sys.M_CS_NSE_ADVISOR

 

 
 
if you are not using advisor, you can run the select * from m_cs_all_columns where table_name = 'RSBATCHDATA' and check memory_size_in_total, for e.g. a column or index which you want to put into NSE

In our system, we saved around 2 TB of memory, which helped a lot in improving the HANA performance and satisfaction of our users.

entry.title: Integrating with SAP Datasphere API through BTP Destination Using OAuth SAML Bearer Assertion
entry.content:
For our proof of concept, we aimed to access the SAP Datasphere API via SAP BTP, utilizing the OAuth2 SAML Bearer authentication flow. Ideally, we wanted to connect directly to this destination through a route in our Approuter. 

We found this blog post helpful as a starting point: Integrating with SAP Datasphere Consumption APIs Using SAML Bearer.  Thanks to @gustavokath 

However, the solution required an additional (NodeJS) middleware. Our goal was to achieve this both with and without a Node.js middleware, leveraging the destination service and default approuter.

We encountered some issues that I would like to document. If our experience can help anyone facing similar challenges along the way, that would be wonderful! 🙂

Prerequisite: Make sure to check out the blog I mentioned earlier, as I won’t be going over all the steps again. I’ll just point out the differences we made.

With NodeJS middleware:

In the scenario where we used a Node.js middleware, we opted for the SAP Cloud SDK instead of handling HTTP requests with Axios. The SAP Cloud SDK simplifies integration with SAP services, providing built-in tools for handling authentication and API interactions. You can find more details in the SAP Cloud SDK documentation.

The following code does the trick:

 

 

 

//Imports
const connectivity = require("@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity");
const httpclient = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client');

//Simple example method to call datasphere destination via Cloud SDK
//Assuming req is a Request object coming from ExpressJS
const callDatasphere = (req, path, params) => {

const jwt = connectivity.retrieveJwt(req);
const destination = await connectivity.getDestination({ destinationName: "datasphere_destination", jwt: jwt });

 let url = `${dest.url}${path}`;
    let result = await httpclient.executeHttpRequest(dest, {
      url: url,
      method: "get",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/xml" },
      params: params
    });

return result.data;
}

 

 

 

Without NodeJS middleware

The above example worked perfectly for us. However, we also wanted to explore an implementation without using a Node.js middleware. In this case, only a managed or custom approuter would be utilized.

For this we added a router to the approuter xs-ap.json file:

While testing this setup, we encountered an unexpected "unauthorized" error. This was puzzling because the destination worked flawlessly when used with the Cloud SDK. To investigate further, we fetched the destination data using Postman. The response confirmed that the tokens were generated correctly, indicating that the OAuth2 SAML Bearer flow was functioning as expected.

While debugging the Approuter, we discovered that it was adding additional headers to the request being sent to the Datasphere API.

One of these headers was "x-forwarded-host", which contained the source domain. By adding an additional property to the destination configuration, we were able to overwrite this header successfully:

uRL.headers.x-forwarded-host: "your-datasphere-domain.com"

 

 

From this point on, we were able to make calls to Datasphere through the Approuter without needing an additional Node.js middleware. This approach also works with a managed Approuter, allowing you to achieve the integration without requiring a Cloud Foundry runtime.

 

Ideally, Datasphere would offer the ability to allowlist our source domains in a way, eliminating the need for the above change in the destination configuration. If you have any ideas on how to achieve this, feel free to share! For now, we will go forward with this work around :-).

SAP Datasphere  SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Use custom Fiori Theme during BAS preview
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

in this brief blog, I’ll walk you through how to preview your custom UI theme in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) during app development. Instead of covering here the steps to create a custom theme, you can refer to this detailed blog: How to create new custom theme in Fiori?

Exporting Your Custom Theme

Once you’ve created your custom theme, the UI Theme Designer allows you to export all its contents as an archive file. To export, simply select your theme and click on the Export option.

In the export dialog, expand the Optional Settings (for Experts) section, and choose the following options:

  • Source Files + CSS Resources + Base Theme Resources
  • SAPUI5 (UI development toolkit for HTML5)

The exported archive contains your custom theme (located under \UI5\sap\fiori\themes), along with the base theme files.

Importing Your Custom Theme into BAS

In BAS, you can import your custom theme into any folder of your choice (for example, into /home/user/projects). To do this, simply drag and drop the folder from your local file explorer directly into the BAS workspace.

Using Your Custom Theme During App Preview
To preview any app in BAS with your custom theme, follow these steps:

1-- In the webapp/test/flpSandbox.html file

Modify the data-sap-ui-theme property by setting it to the ID of your custom theme.
Example:

 

data-sap-ui-theme="demo_theme"

 

Add the data-sap-ui-theme-roots property to define the root path for your theme. Set it using the custom theme ID and an alias of your choice.
Example:

 

data-sap-ui-theme-roots='{"demo_theme" : "myCustomTheme/UI5"}'

 

2-- In the ui5.yaml file

Add a redirection for all network calls related to your custom theme by using the fiori-tools-servestatic middleware.

Example:

 

- name: fiori-tools-servestatic
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        paths:
          - path: /myCustomTheme
            src: /home/user/projects/demo_theme

 

3-- Launch the preview

Launch the app preview by running the webapp/test/flpSandbox.html file to view your custom theme. You can do this by using the command:

 

npm run start

 

And voilà! You custom theme is visible during the app preview in BAS:

Known Issue with SAP Fonts
Depending on your version of the UI Theme Designer, you may encounter issues with certain icons disappearing. This occurs because these icons rely on SAP fonts, which are not managed correctly during export in UI Theme Designer versions prior to 1.63. This problem is discussed here: UI5 Theme Designer: Failed to decode downloaded font

 

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: SAP ABAP New Syntax
entry.content:
After version 7.4 in SAP, new ABAP syntaxes where introduced which helps developer increases the efficiency of the code and also makes it easy to write and handle, which includes Inline Declaration, Table Expression, Data Operations.

Here are some of the new syntaxes which I have come across and found out to be very useful for working in ABAP and make program less complex.

Inline Declarations:
Before 7.4 New syntaxes were not introduced the variable needs to be declared before using it, now using inline declaration variable can we declare and use at the same time, Below are some of the syntax which has changed after 7.4.

Whenever data is to be moved to a variable, can be declared at the same instance,

Old Syntax.
 DATA var1 TYPE char5.
 var1 = ‘ABC’. 

New Syntax.
 DATA(var1) = ‘ABC’. 

Select Single from Database Table into Work Area

Old Syntax.
DATA wa TYPE ....
SELECT SINGLE fld1 fld2 FROM ....
INTO wa
WHERE fld1 = var1
AND fld2 = var2. 

New Syntax.
SELECT SINGLE fld1, fld2, FROM ....
INTO @DATA(wa)
WHERE fld1 = @var1
AND fld2 = @var2.

Selecting Data from Database Table into Internal Table,

Old Syntax.
DATA itab TYPE TABLE OF ....
SELECT fld1 fld2 FROM ....
INTO TABLE itab
WHERE fld1 = var1
AND fld2 = var2. 

New Syntax.
SELECT fld1, fld2 FROM ....
INTO TABLE @DATA(itab)
WHERE fld1 = @var1
AND fld2 = @var2. 

Declaration of the work area while operation on Internal Table,

Old Syntax.
DATA wa TYPE structure
LOOP AT itab INTO wa.
or
READ TABLE itab into wa WITH KEY field1 = var1. 

New Syntax.
LOOP AT itab INTO data(wa).
or
READ TABLE itab into data(wa) WITH KEY field1 = var1. 

Moving Data from One Table to Other.

Old Syntax.
DATA lt_tab LIKE itab.
lt_tab[] = itab[]. 

New Syntax.
DATA(lt_tab) = itab[]. 

Assigning Field Symbols

Old Syntax.
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <line> type …
LOOP AT itab ASSIGNING <line>.
or
READ TABLE itab ASSIGNING <line> WITH KEY FLD1 = VAR1. 

New Syntax.
LOOP AT itab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>).
OR
READ TABLE itab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>) WITH KEY FLD1 = VAR1. 

Table Expression:
In SAP ABAP, Whenever data is to be taken from Internal Table to any variable in Code for further processing, Loop or Read Table are generally used to get that data from Internal Table to work area and then move to another variable, so this effort can be lessened by using the new syntax.

Get line from table to Work Area.

Old Syntax.
READ TABLE itab INTO DATA(wa) WITH KEY fld1 = var1. 

New Syntax.
DATA(wa) = itab[ fld1 = var1 ]. 

Read table with Index 1.

Old Syntax.
READ TABLE itab INTO DATA(wa) index 1. 

New Syntax.
DATA(wa) = itab[ 1 ]. 

Get a particular field from one table to another variable.

Old Syntax.
READ TABLE itab INTO wa WITH KEY fld1 = var1.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lv_var2 = wa-var2.
ENDIF. 

New Syntax.
lv_var2 = itab[ fld1 = var1 ]-var2. 

Check particular Value is in Internal Table.

Old Syntax.
READ TABLE itab TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS WITH KEY fld1 = var1.
IF sy-subrc = 0.ENDIF. 

New Syntax.
IF line_exists( itab[ fld1 = var1 ] ).ENDIF. 

Get Index Number of Particular Entry from Internal Table.

Old Syntax.
READ TABLE itab TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS fld1 = var1.
IF sy-subrc eq 0
lv_tabix = sy-tabix.
ENDIF. 

New Syntax.
lv_tabix = line_index( itab[ fld1 = var1 ] ). 

Modify Entry in Table.

Old Syntax.
READ TABLE itab ASSIGNING <lfs_tab> fld1 = var1.
IF sy-subrc eq 0
<lfs_tab>-fld2 = 'Text'.
ENDIF. 

New Syntax.
itab[ fld1 = var1 ]-fld2 = 'Text'. 

Note: While Using these Syntaxes in case of Table Expression use with Try <> Catch with cx_sy_itab_line_not_found EndTry block.

Data Operations:
Here are some of the new Syntax which can be used for manipulating, changing, and storing data according to requirement.

Adding or Removing Leading Zeros to a Variable.

Old Syntax.
For Removing Leading Zeros
CALL FUNCTION ‘CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_OUTPUT’
 EXPORTING
 input = var1
 IMPORTING
 OUTPUT = var1 .
For Adding Leading Zeros
CALL FUNCTION ‘CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT’
 EXPORTING
 input = var1
 IMPORTING
 OUTPUT = var1 . 

New Syntax.
For Removing Leading Zeros
var1 = |{ var1 ALPHA = OUT}|.
For Adding Leading Zeros
var1 = |{ var1 ALPHA = IN }|. 

Getting Data into Variable Depending on Condition.

Old Syntax.
IF cond1.
 var1 = fld1.
ELSE.
 var1 = fld2.
ENDIF. 

New Syntax.
var1 = COND #( WHEN cond1 THEN fld1 ELSE fld2 ). 

Case EndCase.

Old Syntax.
CASE var1.
WHEN cond1.
 var1 = fld1.
WHEN cond2.
 var1 = fld2.
ENDCASE. 

New Syntax.
var1 = SWITCH #( var1 WHEN cond1 THEN fld1
                      WHEN cond2 THEN fld2 ). 

Creating Table and Filling Data into a Table

Old Syntax.
DATA: itab TYPE TABLE OF structure,
      wa TYPE structure. 

wa-fld1 = 1.
wa-fld2 = 'A'.
APPEND wa TO itab.
CLEAR WA. 

wa-fld1 = 2.
wa-fld2 = 'B'.
APPEND wa TO itab.
CLEAR WA. 

New Syntax.
DATA(itab) = VALUE structure(( fld1 = 1 fld2 = 'A' )
                             ( fld1 = 2 fld2 = 'B' )). 

Adding New entries to Internal Table already having entries.

Old Syntax.
DATA: itab TYPE TABLE OF structure,
      wa TYPE structure. 

wa-fld1 = 1.
wa-fld2 = 'A'.
APPEND wa TO itab.
CLEAR WA. 

wa-fld1 = 2.
wa-fld2 = 'B'.

APPEND wa TO itab.
CLEAR WA. 

New Syntax.
itab = VALUE #( BASE itab
          ( fld1 = 1 fld2 = 'A' )
          ( fld1 = 2 fld2 = 'B' ) ). 

Concatenate for data type apart from C, N, D, T, or STRING. Before 7.4 use to get Syntax Error saying this, but with new Syntax, it’s possible for any Data Type.

Old Syntax.
CONCATENATE 'The Value in inr is' var1 into fld1. 

New Syntax.
fld1 = |The Value in inr is { var1 }|. 

Combining two tables into a single table.

Old Syntax
loop at itab1 into wa1.
 append wa1 into itab3.
endloop.loop at itab2 into wa2.
 append wa2 into itab3.
endloop.

New Syntax.
itab3 = itab2.
itab3 = corresponding #( BASE ( itab3 ) itab2 ).

Moving Data from one Container to another.

Old Syntax.
MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 to itab2. 

New Syntax.
itab2 = CORRESPONDING #( itab1 ). 

Using above Syntax only fields that are identical in the container will be moved to move it to other fields this new syntax can be used.

itab2 = CORRESPONDING #( itab1 MAPPING t1_fld1 = t2_fld1
                                       t1_fld2 = t2_fld2 ).

If some fields need to excluded while moving corresponding fields, this syntax can be used.

itab2 = CORRESPONDING #( itab1 EXCEPT t1_fld3,t1_fld4 ).

Moving Data from Table to other by Splitting it into two fields.

 *--Defining Structure
TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_rseg_key,
        belnr TYPE belnr_d,
        gjahr TYPE gjahr,
       END OF gty_s_rseg,
    gty_rseg TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF gty_s_rseg WITH EMPTY KEY. 

Old Syntax.
DATA: lt_awkey TYPE gty_rseg,
      lw_awkey TYPE gty_s_rseg. 

LOOP AT lt_bkpf into lw_bkpf.
 lw_awkey-belnr = lw_bkpf-awkey+0(10).
 lw_awkey-gjahr = lw_bkpf-awkey+10(4).
 APPEND lw_awkey to lt_awkey.

ENDLOOP. 

New Syntax.
DATA(lt_awkey) = VALUE gty_rseg( FOR <lfs_bkpf> IN lt_bkpf
                               ( belnr = <lfs_bkpf>-awkey+0(10)
                                 gjahr = <lfs_bkpf>-awkey+10(4) ) ). 

Display Message combining two or more Fileds.

Old Syntax.
DATA lv_name TYPE sy-ucomm.
DATA lv_output TYPE char20.
CONCATENATE 'The User ID is' lv_name INTO lv_output SEPARATED BY space.
MESSAGE lv_output TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'. 

New Syntax.
MESSAGE |The User ID is { lv_name }| TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'. 

Object-Oriented.

Old Syntax.
DATA: binary_content TYPE solix_tab.
DATA: xl_content TYPE xstring . 

CALL METHOD cl_document_bcs=>xstring_to_solix
EXPORTING
ip_xstring = xl_content
receiving
rt_solix = binary_content. 

New Syntax.
binary_content = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_solix( ip_xstring = xl_content ). 

Creating Object for OOABAP.

Old Syntax.
DATA oref TYPE REF TO class.
CREATE OBJECT oref EXPORTING … 

New Syntax.
DATA oref TYPE REF TO class.
oref = NEW #( … ).
or
with an inline declaration
DATA(oref) = NEW class( … ) 

 

Thank you for reading. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: Hi, I am unable to find onboarding practice system in...
